title: Deep-Fried Poblanos Tlayudas
description: Nutty, velvety Mexican dish featuring blackeye peas, poblanos, and a crispy agemono-fry. Perfect for a post-workout meal.
instructions: Heat vegetable oil in a deep frying pan to 350°F (175°C).
Cut poblanos into 1-inch pieces and set aside.
In a bowl, mix together cornmeal, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Dip each poblano piece into the cornmeal mixture, coating evenly.
Fry the coated poblanos until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per piece.
Drain the fried poblanos on paper towels.
In a separate pan, heat a tablespoon of vegetable oil over medium heat.
Add garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add blackeye peas and cook until heated through, about 2-3 minutes.
Warm the ti leaves by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the tlayudas by placing a fried poblano on each ti leaf, followed by a spoonful of blackeye peas and a sprinkle of garlic.
Fold the ti leaves to enclose the filling and serve immediately.
